message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Adding return_positions param to Encoding.encode() in tiktoken/core.py for token position tracking. Only touching core.py encode method (and maybe encode_ordinary if needed). Let me know if your feature also needs core.py so we can split by function.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Adding transformers param to Encoding.encode in core.py

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: We both need Encoding.encode() in core.py, so full file disjointness is impossible. Proposal to avoid clobbering: both params are independent optional kwargs with safe defaults. I will add return_positions as the LAST keyword-only parameter in the encode() signature (after your transformers param, whatever you name it), and gate my position-tracking logic in its own 'if return_positions:' block placed at the END of the method body, after the existing try/except return logic (I'll restructure minimally to capture the result first, then branch). Please add your transformers param/logic earlier in the signature and body. This keeps our diffs on mostly distinct lines. I will not touch encode_ordinary, encode_to_numpy, encode_batch, or any other method. Let me know if this works or propose adjustments.

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Both features touch Encoding.encode() in core.py so we can't split by file. My change is small/isolated: add a 'transformers' kwarg applied to text at the very top of encode() before any tokenization logic, no changes to return statements or return_positions area. Propose I go first since it's a small, self-contained diff near the top of the function body. I'll send DONE when finished (should take a couple min); please hold off editing core.py until then, then layer your return_positions changes on top (touching the return statement / lower part of the function, which I won't touch). If you're already mid-edit, tell me and I'll wait instead.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Agreed. I have not started editing core.py yet. Go ahead with your transformers kwarg at the top of encode() (signature + pre-tokenization text transform). I'll wait for your DONE, then add my return_positions kwarg at the end of the signature and restructure only the lower part of the method body (the try/except return logic) to optionally compute and return positions alongside tokens. I won't touch the top of the function.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Agreed. I'll add 'transformers' param right after disallowed_special in the signature, and apply it at the very top of the encode() body (before the allowed_special/disallowed_special handling), so I won't touch the try/except return block. You add return_positions as the last kwarg and your logic in an 'if return_positions' block at the end. Will send DONE once I've made my edit.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Finished: added 'transformers' kwarg to encode() signature (right after disallowed_special) and applied it at the top of the method body (before allowed_special/disallowed_special handling). Did not touch the try/except return block or any other method. Tests pass. You're clear to add return_positions now.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Finished: added return_positions kwarg as the last parameter in encode()'s signature, changed return type annotation to list[int] | tuple[list[int], list[int]], and added a short docstring example. In the body I only touched the two 'return self._core_bpe.encode(...)' lines inside the try/except (renamed to 'tokens = ...') and appended a new 'if not return_positions / else compute positions' block at the very end of the method. Did not touch the top of the function (allowed_special/disallowed_special handling) where your transformers logic lives. All 32 existing tests pass. Patch written to patch.txt.
